title: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
summary: Starfish Space has secured a new partner for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner withdrew. The company adjusted its plans following the unexpected change in partnership.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
summary: The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida on March 27 for final preparations ahead of a planned launch as soon as April 1. This marks a key milestone in NASA's Artemis program, which aims to return humans to the Moon.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of two new associate members, Astrolab and Zeno Power, which focus on planetary mobility and energy solutions for extreme environments. This expansion highlights CSF's growing influence in the commercial space sector.

title: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
summary: ESA is set to decide by June on Europe's contributions to the lunar Gateway project, which has faced delays and changes in NASA's Artemis program. The decision comes after NASA halted work on the Gateway station in March.

title: India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive
summary: India-based Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million to expand production of satellite propulsion systems after securing its first major overseas commercial customer. The funding follows the company's push to grow internationally.

title: Building a hypersonic pathway to orbit with Mark Bigham
summary: Mark Bigham of Longshot Space Technologies Corporation discusses hypersonic pathways to orbit on the Space Minds podcast. The conversation highlights the company's new hardware startup initiatives in the space industry.

title: The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains
summary: Satellite technology has revolutionized precision agriculture and supply chain monitoring, but it cannot fully address the 'ground truth' gap in AgTech. AI and analytics enhance satellite data, but additional methods are needed for accurate supply chain tracking.

title: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
summary: NASA's proposed changes to its support for commercial space stations have caused concern and confusion among developing companies. Industry leaders warn that these adjustments may disrupt ongoing plans.

title: SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit
summary: Canadian startup SBQuantum will send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit on March 30 aboard a Spire Global satellite launched by SpaceX's Falcon 9. Spire is providing the satellite platform, ground stations, and data processing for the mission.

title: ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service
summary: ispace is revising its lunar lander design and delaying its first mission by its American subsidiary while also announcing plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

title: Where should we send a real 'Hail Mary' spacecraft? A new study has the answers
summary: A new study has identified 45 potentially habitable planets as potential targets for a future 'Hail Mary' spacecraft mission. The research draws inspiration from Andy Weir's novel 'Project Hail Mary,' which is being adapted into a film.

title: Astrophotographer captures spectacular photo of Antennae Galaxies dueling in deep space
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of the Antennae Galaxies merging into a single elliptical galaxy. This rare cosmic event highlights the dynamic processes of galactic collisions.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 203 — China Rising
summary: Episode 203 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses China's rising influence in space and potential threats to American leadership, featuring guest Dr. Namrata Goswami from Johns Hopkins University.

title: This Celestron Firstscope is my go-to for moonwatching and it's 15% off in Amazon's Spring Sale
summary: A Celestron Firstscope telescope is featured as an ideal tool for moonwatching and is currently 15% off during Amazon's Spring Sale. The compact tabletop telescope is recommended for both adults and children.

title: How to watch NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts launch to the moon on April 1
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission is scheduled to launch four astronauts to the moon on April 1 at 6:24 p.m. EDT (2224 GMT). The event will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at Kennedy Space Center ahead of NASA's historic launch around the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have arrived at Kennedy Space Center ahead of their historic lunar flyby mission, scheduled to launch in five days.

title: What time is NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch on April 1?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed moon flight in over 50 years, is scheduled to launch on April 1. The event will be broadcast live for public viewing.

title: Still waiting for Ahsoka Season 2? Make the wait easier with 20% off this Lego Star Wars Ahsoka Tano Jedi Interceptor
summary: A Lego Star Wars Ahsoka Tano Jedi Interceptor set is being offered at a 20% discount at Walmart, providing a distraction while fans wait for Star Wars: Ahsoka Season 2.

title: Can humans have babies in space? It may be harder than expected
summary: A new study suggests that mammalian reproduction in space may be impossible due to sperm struggling to reach eggs in microgravity. This finding challenges the feasibility of human reproduction beyond Earth.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon. His career has focused on enabling spacecraft communication with Earth.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA has selected Intuitive Machines to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon for $180.4 million under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission includes seven payloads, five of which are NASA-funded, to enhance lunar surface understanding.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the categories for its Environment and Energy 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management, compliance, conservation, and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in stewardship, remediation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's wide swath imagery covered the Pacific Northwest region.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens in Washington on November 10, 2025. NISAR's L-band SAR instrument successfully peered through clouds to reveal the surface below.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The payloads aim to test thermal protection systems, improve in-space communications, and enhance understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has chosen 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including instrument deployment and sample collection. The team will help guide astronauts in conducting critical observations and experiments during Moon missions.

title: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
summary: Michael Guzman, Artemis II main propulsion systems engineer, shares insights on his work and passion for rocket science through his email signature. His role involves critical propulsion systems for NASA's Artemis missions.

title: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad
summary: NASA plans to release a BAA under NextSTEP-3 Appendix E for Project NEXUS, aiming to acquire a Ka-band backward-compatible relay service to replace the aging TDRS system. The project includes space, ground, launch, integration, and operations components.

title: Artemis II Crew’s Suits
summary: NASA and CSA unveil the Orion Crew Survival System suits for the Artemis II crew, including Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen, ahead of their test flight.

title: SpaceX to deliver 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch 119 payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit as part of its Transporter 16 mission. The launch is scheduled to take place from California.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA revealed detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, marking a significant shift in its lunar exploration strategy. The agency is pivoting away from the Lunar Gateway project in favor of direct Moon base development.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch but faced an unexpected delay.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the plan.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is rapidly advancing construction on Starbase launch pads to support the upcoming Block 3 Starship configuration, highlighting efforts to increase launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including the U.S., Russia, China, New Zealand, and Norway, are preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made to the launch site after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space. This marks a significant expansion of the company's ambitions beyond launch services.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 ahead of its planned launch.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website has been mentioned as a source for space industry news. No specific details about the news content are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. This publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber, with 26 ultra-sensitive cameras exposed to extreme temperatures and vacuum. The spacecraft is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, requiring all suppliers to use it for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research and hands-on learning in space science and technology.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology. The company aims to provide stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman marks 100 days in office with an announcement emphasizing a return to the Moon. The statement suggests renewed focus on lunar missions.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes may leave them at a disadvantage. Europe now faces uncertainty regarding its role in the Artemis program.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's evolving vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has sparked confusion and debate among legislators.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to space-based interceptors (SBIs). It highlights its potential advantages over traditional methods.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions to streamline regulatory authority. This initiative aims to address gaps in existing frameworks for missions that don't fit traditional categories.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to develop in-space propulsion technology using storable bipropellants, which are designed to simplify launch operations and reduce costs compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. The company aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital missions rise.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman's vision for a commercially driven lunar economy has evolved with a new plan. This shift aims to support surface infrastructure for future lunar activities.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's approach to solving satellite ground infrastructure challenges with a rapidly deployable end-to-end ground system. The company aims to address the complexities of satellite communication infrastructure.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The discussion highlights advancements in this technology sector.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market outlook amid volatility. Discussions focused on direct-to-direct (D2D) strategies, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discussed the company's RF solutions for LEO satellites during SATShow Week. The interview highlighted Qorvo's phased array technologies and other satellite communication advancements.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are increasingly seeking stronger control over their own satellite communications (Satcom) networks due to geopolitical uncertainties. The challenge lies in defining what 'sovereign space' means and its capabilities within a more complex global space infrastructure.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Investors discuss the need for improved financing routes, including IPOs, to support the growing commercial space sector. Venture capitalists highlight that funding mechanisms must evolve to match the industry's rapid technological advancements.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking data from space to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the satellite industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a competitive offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now expanding their networks to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulatory frameworks are expected to drive the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. These trends will shape the industry's development as key factors.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems and live multi-orbit demonstrations during SATShow Week.
